title: 10 best herbal essential oils for varicose veins
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for varicose veins are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms and promote overall vein health. These oils are obtained from various plant extracts and are effective in reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and strengthening vein walls. Some of the best herbal essential oils for varicose veins include Hamamelis virginiana, Ginkgo biloba, Ruscus aculeatus, Vitis vinifera, Aesculus hippocastanum, Crataegus monogyna, Vaccinium myrtillus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Centella asiatica, and Equisetum arvense. If left untreated, varicose veins can cause pain, swelling, and discomfort, significantly impacting daily life, limiting mobility, and affecting overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its unique properties that aid in improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and strengthening vein walls. The astringent and antiseptic qualities of witch hazel help to constrict the skin's pores, reducing swelling and promoting healthy tissue growth around affected areas. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds reduce discomfort and pain associated with varicose veins, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Tightens skin and reduces inflammation, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins such as swelling and itching.
Antioxidant property: Neutralizes free radicals and protects the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of varicose veins.
Vasoconstrictive property: Narrows blood vessels and reduces their diameter, which can help alleviate symptoms such as painful swelling and bruising associated with varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it effective in treating varicose veins.
Roots: Containing saponins, these help to strengthen capillary walls and reduce swelling associated with varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours using paper bags.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus for 4-6 hours with 10 kg of distilled water.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with varicose veins because it contains flavonoids, which help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and discomfort associated with varicose veins. Additionally, bitter orange essential oil's ability to stimulate the lymphatic system helps to remove toxins and excess fluids that can contribute to vein issues. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation, this natural remedy can provide effective relief from varicose vein symptoms.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictive properties: Bitter orange essential oil helps to constrict blood vessels, which can improve circulation and reduce swelling associated with varicose veins.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by varicose veins, promoting a more comfortable and healthy circulatory system.

Antioxidant properties: Bitter orange essential oil contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage blood vessels and contribute to the development of varicose veins, thus helping to protect against further vein damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant are used to make essential oil, which helps to improve circulation and reduce inflammation associated with varicose veins.
Seeds: The seeds of the bitter orange plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort caused by varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried citrus peel from mature trees in the morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the peel further by spreading it out on paper towels and letting it air dry for 7 days.
Combine 100 grams of dried citrus peel with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar, seal tightly, and steep for 14 days.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids, and add 200 milliliters of distilled water to create a 30% ethanol solution.
Distill the liquid using a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure until 100 milliliters of essential oil is collected, which may take approximately 4 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its remarkable ability to improve blood circulation. The oil's properties help to dilate blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing swelling and discomfort associated with varicose veins. Additionally, geranium oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and pain in the affected areas, providing relief from the often painful symptoms of varicose veins. Its natural anticoagulant properties also promote healthy blood clotting, further contributing to its beneficial effects on vein health.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Geranium essential oil has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent bacterial infection and reduce inflammation associated with varicose veins.

Astringent: The astringent properties of geranium essential oil can help constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as swelling and discomfort.

Tonic: Geranium essential oil has tonic properties that can help regulate blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of developing varicose veins or alleviating their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of geranium are used to make essential oils that help alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins, such as inflammation and pain.
Barks: Geranium bark contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers from organic sources weighing about 100 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for at least seven days or until it reaches a moisture content of 10 percent.
Steam distill the dried pelargonium graveolens material with deionized water in a ratio of 1:5 for four hours to obtain essential oil.
Monitor and record the essential oil production every 30 minutes during the steam distillation process ensuring optimal yield.
Filter the obtained essential oil through paper or cloth to remove any impurities then transfer it into dark glass bottles for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort essential oil helps with varicose veins because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When applied topically, it reduces swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, relieving discomfort and pain associated with varicose veins. Its antioxidant properties also help to improve blood circulation, reducing pressure on the veins and promoting healthy vein function. Additionally, St John's Wort essential oil has a soothing effect on the skin, calming irritation and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort essential oil helps to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of varicose veins.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in St. John's Wort essential oil, such as hyperforin and adenol, help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as swelling, pain, and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort essential oil has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body, which can reduce inflammation and edema associated with varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with varicose veins.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit vasoprotective effects, helping to strengthen vein walls and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers in sufficient quantity for distillation.
Dry the collected material at room temperature for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Load the dried flowers into a distillation apparatus and prepare the condenser coils.
Distill the herbs using steam at a pressure of 1 atm for 4 hours to extract essential oils.
Transfer the extracted oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place for 14 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its natural ability to improve circulation. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on varicose veins and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, rosemary essential oil's ability to stimulate the lymphatic system promotes the removal of toxins and waste products that can contribute to vein congestion. Regular use of rosemary essential oils may help to reduce symptoms such as swelling, pain, and discoloration associated with varicose veins.

Improves circulation: Rosemary essential oil's vasodilatory effects help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, alleviating symptoms associated with varicose veins such as swelling and discomfort.

Barks: The bark of the rosemary plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce swelling, making it a valuable addition to essential oils for treating varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal oil quality and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested leaves in small bunches tied together using twine or string in a warm well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves at 100°c with distilled water in a 1:5 ratio for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil from impurities using a coffee filter paper or glass wool and store in dark glass bottles.
Mix the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 10% concentration for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with this condition. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation also plays a crucial role in alleviating symptoms, as it promotes the free flow of blood through the veins. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich properties of pot marigold essential oils help protect the veins from damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to its effectiveness in relieving varicose vein-related discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning or evening when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine one cup of dried calendula officinalis with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm and dark place at room temperature for four to six weeks, shaking occasionally, allowing the botanicals to infuse into the oil.

Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the calendula essential oil to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with varicose veins because it has a unique ability to promote circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax the walls of blood vessels. Its antispasmodic properties help to ease cramping and discomfort associated with varicose veins, while its antioxidant properties protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress. remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves by cutting fresh sprigs from mature plants at dawn to capture maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in small batches using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 48 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 using a steam distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decanting the mixture and storing the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Age the essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 24 hours to allow the oil to mature and stabilize its constituents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon essential oils helps with varicose veins because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with this condition. Additionally, lemon oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax and ease muscle cramps, a common symptom of varicose veins. remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried citrus peels and 1 cup of calendula flowers for the infusion process.
Steam distill the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f for 3 hours, collecting the resulting oil.
Combine 10 drops of citral-rich lemon essential oil with 20 drops of calendula oil for a synergistic blend.
Mix 5ml of the blended oil with 95% glycerin and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil for an emollient serum.
Store the finished serum in dark glass bottles, allowing it to mature for 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its unique blend of properties. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Eucalyptus globulus contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of varicose veins.

Antithrombotic properties: Eucalyptus globulus has been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation and prevent the formation of blood clots, which can reduce the risk of developing varicose veins or worsening existing ones.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it effective in treating varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse and dry the harvested leaves by air drying them for 3-4 days.
Steam distill 200g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water using a separation funnel and discard the plant material.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove impurities before storing it in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Potato essential oils helps with varicose veins because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that improve blood circulation, reduce swelling, and ease discomfort. remedy_properties: Cirsimaritin: A flavonoid found in Centella asiatica (Centella) oil, which has been shown to improve blood vessel elasticity and reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.
Hesperidin: A bioflavonoid found in Citrus sinensis (Orange) oil, which can help strengthen capillary walls and improve circulation, reducing the appearance of varicose veins.

Cajeputol: A sesquiterpene found in Melaleuca quinquenervia (Cajeput) oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh solanum tuberosum leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing approximately one kilogram.
Wash the harvested material gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris allowing 30 minutes for soaking.
Steam distill the washed solanum tuberosum material at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius with a ratio of 1:4 solvent to plant material for two hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil by separating it from the distilled water and filter it using paper or cloth filters allowing one hour for sedimentation.
Store the final herbal solanum tuberosum essential oil in dark glass bottles at a temperature range of 10 to 20 degrees celsius with a shelf life of six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat varicose veins with herbal essential oils is to combine rosemary and lavender oils in a massage blend. Mix 5 drops of rosemary oil with 3 drops of lavender oil and apply topically to the affected area using gentle circular motions. This combination helps improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to varicose veins that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions characterized by poor circulation, inflammation, and discomfort. Herbal essential oils such as Geranium, Lavender, and Peppermint can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids, spider veins, and phlebitis. These oils have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healthy blood flow when used topically or through aromatherapy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of blood clots helps to alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins, such as pain, swelling, and discoloration. Additionally, its antioxidant properties promote healthy blood circulation, which can help to reduce the appearance of varicose veins. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin and promote overall vascular health.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: It helps with varicose veins by acting as a vasoconstrictor, which reduces blood flow to the affected area and helps alleviate symptoms.
Quercetin: It helps with varicose veins by inhibiting the breakdown of collagen and improving vascular health, which can help reduce the appearance of varicose veins.
Gallic acid: It helps with varicose veins by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Hamamelis virginiana leaves are used to make essential oils due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate varicose vein symptoms.

Barks: Hamamelis virginiana barks are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of hamamelitannin, a polyphenolic compound with astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel health.

Fruits (seeds and fruits): Hamamelis virginiana seeds and fruits are used to make essential oils due to their high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce varicose vein symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs when the plant is in full bloom at dawn.
Steam distill 100g of fresh plant material with 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities.
Store the hamamelis virginiana essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with varicose veins because it enhances blood circulation and improves oxygen delivery to the skin. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in the oil help to strengthen blood vessels and improve vein health. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with varicose veins. remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides help with varicose veins by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), which is involved in the inflammation and clotting associated with varicose veins.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide helps with varicose veins by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids help with varicose veins by improving blood vessel function and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling associated with varicose veins.

Buds: The buds are used due to their rich content of ginkgolides and flavonoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of ginkgo biloba leaves from the tree when they are young and fresh.
Dry the leaves in a cool dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and debris.

The oil contains a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other compounds that help to strengthen and tone the veins, reducing the appearance of varicose veins. remedy_recipe: Harvest ruscus aculeatus leaves in early morning when they are at their highest concentration of essential oils.
Steam distill 1 kg of harvested ruscus aculeatus leaves with 3 liters of water for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a separator for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil using activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its quality for 10 minutes.
Store the ruscus aculeatus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used to make essential oils that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their highest concentration of essential oil.

Steam distill the dried vitis vinifera leaves and flowers at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 4-6 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum essential oils helps with varicose veins because it is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and vascular-protective properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aesculus hippocastanum leaves and flowers at dawn when they are heavy with dew and moisture.

Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Crataegus monogyna are used to make essential oils due to their high content of flavonoids, which are believed to improve circulation and reduce inflammation.

Collect the essential oil that condenses on the inner surface of the distillation apparatus and transfer it to a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil, derived from bilberries, contains anthocyanins and flavonoids that improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen blood vessel walls. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, Vaccinium myrtillus essential oils can help alleviate the symptoms of varicose veins, such as swelling, pain, and discoloration, leading to improved circulation and overall venous health.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: Help to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the formation and symptoms of varicose veins.
Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can cause blood vessels to become damaged and lead to varicose veins.

Flavonoids: Help to improve blood vessel function, reduce inflammation, and prevent the breakdown of collagen in blood vessel walls, which can contribute to the formation and symptoms of varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Vaccinium myrtillus leaves are used to make essential oils for varicose veins due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and improve circulation.

Fruits: Vaccinium myrtillus fruits are used to make essential oils for varicose veins due to their high anthocyanin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Vaccinium myrtillus stems are used to make essential oils for varicose veins due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and improve circulation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with varicose veins because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected areas. This, in turn, can alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discoloration associated with varicose veins. The oil's ability to strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation also contributes to its effectiveness in addressing varicose veins. Regular use may lead to improved vein health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants may help strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation, reducing the appearance and discomfort of varicose veins.
Liquiritigenin: This isoflavonoid may have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing of varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used because they are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and swelling associated with varicose veins.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and calm varicose veins.
Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are utilized for their oil content, which is rich in fatty acids and has anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief for varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the roots in a clevenger apparatus with 100g of water at 100c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a separating funnel for 5 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities for 10 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica essential oils helps with varicose veins because it promotes blood circulation and reduces inflammation, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with varicose veins. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties enhance skin elasticity and improve blood vessel health, reducing the appearance of spider veins and varicose veins. Its ability to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation also helps to reduce swelling and promote healing, providing relief from the symptoms of varicose veins. Regular use may also improve skin tone and texture.
remedy_constituents: Brassinins: Help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.
Triterpenoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to protect the veins from damage and promote healing.
Asiatic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to reduce swelling and promote the repair of damaged veins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Centella asiatica leaves are the primary part used to make essential oils for varicose veins because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds that help in reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation.

Roots: Centella asiatica roots are also used to make essential oils for varicose veins due to their rich content of triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in healing and soothing the veins.

Stems: Centella asiatica stems are sometimes used to make essential oils for varicose veins, as they contain a mixture of triterpenoids and saponins that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems, clean them thoroughly with water.
Steam distill the leaves and stems at 100c for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a 50-micron filter paper.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense essential oils helps with varicose veins because it has been traditionally used to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to relax the muscles in the legs and reduce pressure on the veins. By improving circulation and reducing pressure, Equisetum arvense essential oils may help to alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as pain, swelling, and discoloration. It may also help to promote healing and reduce the appearance of varicose veins.
remedy_constituents: Fisetin: A flavonoid that helps to improve blood vessel tone and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins.
Quercetin: A polyphenolic flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and promote the healing of damaged blood vessels.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps to improve blood vessel health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins.
